title: Unable to run in pod
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm attempting to create a pod and run an image inside with a uidmap.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `mkdir db`

2. `podman pod create --name mongo-pod`

3. `podman run --rm -it --pod mongo-pod --uidmap 0:1:999 --uidmap 999:0:1 -v ./db:/data/db:z mongo:3`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:58: mounting \\\"sysfs\\\" to rootfs \\\"/home/zeranoe/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/7ec60f5804c2b8dc282b4ef4fbf03e4a15e97e2caaf9c8b14cf404fd59cd54b5\\\" at \\\"/sys\\\" caused \\\"operation not permitted\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The image should be started inside the pod.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This only seems to happen on my Pi 4, as the same commands work fine on a Fedora 32 system. It also seems to be related to the pod somehow, as `podman run --rm -it --uidmap 0:1:999 --uidmap 999:0:1 -v ./db:/data/db:z mongo:3` works fine. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~2 arm64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.1.1~2 armhf
podman/unknown 2.1.1~2 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Only seems to happen on a Pi 4 running 64-bit Ubuntu 20.04.

comments.body:
We don't have support for running usernamespaces within pod's yet.
The way this should work is you setup the pod to run within a user namespace and then you add containers.



podman pod create  --uidmap 0:1:999 --uidmap 999:0:1 --name mongo-pod
podman run --rm -it --pod mongo-pod -v ./db:/data/db:z mongo:3

Sadly this does not work right now.
The same set of commands work on Fedora 32, so I'm curious how that's possible?
@rhatdan So we don't support running *all* containers in a pod in a user namespace, but individual containers should be able to join one. Any possibility this is the caps we dropped?

No I don't see how that would effect this.

First off why are you using vfs rather then overlay?  Do you have fuse-overlayfs installed?

overlay was not installed, but is now:
```

  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs

  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:

      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.0
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
        FUSE library version 3.9.0
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/zeranoe/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:

    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 3
  runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers

I recreated ~/.local and retried, but unfortunately this results in the same error.

@giuseppe Would you expect this to work.
Basically you have a container running in a user namespace that is different then the infra containers user namespace.
@mheon what namespaces are we sharing by default within pods?
IPC means we would share the shm directory between the pause container and the new container, and this could be owned by a UID that is not allowed.

It works on Fedora most likely because you are using crun instead of runc.  Could you try if it works with crun instead of using runc?

On a new enough kernel (5.3+), crun uses the new mount API to grab a reference to the mount from the old container and mount it in the new container.

That was it, it works with `podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run ...`.

Thank you!
